rulings.Case Title: EVA ARMADA ET AL VS. MICHAEL GUMBAN PANGILINAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Depo Of Remy Cabrera And For Monetary Sanctions And For Terminating Sanctions Against Irene Pangilinan
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 26, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF EVA ARMADA, ANTONIO SALSEDO, RUDY CONCEPCION, LORNA BINALEY's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Remy Cabrera And For Monetary Sanctions And For Terminating Sanctions Against Irene Pangilinan. Pro Tem Judge Maxwell Pritt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' unopposed Motion To Compel the Deposition of Remy Cabrera and for Monetary Sanctions and for Terminating Sanctions Against Irene Pangilinan is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant Cabrera is ordered to appear for deposition no later than Friday, July 12, 2019 at a mutually agreed-upon date, time, and location. If the parties cannot agree to any of those logistical details, then Defendant Cabrera must appear for deposition at a date, time, and location set by Plaintiffs between July 8 and July 12, 2019. Plaintiffs' motion for monetary sanctions against Defendant Cabrera is denied because Defendant Cabrera did not fail to appear but rather the parties could not agree upon a date for the deposition. Plaintiffs' motion for sanctions against Defendant Pangilinan is denied as moot because, according to Plaintiffs' counsel's June 19, 2019 declaration, Defendant Pangilinan appeared for deposition on June 13, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mpritt@bsfllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO MONTEAGUDO ET AL VS. GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Production By California Department Of Justice, Cures 2.0 Program
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 26, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, SF 267 GREEN STREET, LLC's Motion For Order Requiring Production By California Department Of Justice, Cures 2.0 Program. CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion for Order Requiring Production By California Department of Justice, Cures 2.0 Program is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The Court authorizes the Department of Justice to release subpoenaed CURES data to Defendant as required by CURES General Conditions, No. 9, but only in the event that Plaintiff does not stipulate to the issues set forth in California Code of Civil Procedure section 2032.320(c)(1) & (2) within 30 days of the date of Notice of Entry of this Order. If Plaintiff so stipulates, then Defendant must withdraw its subpoena to the Department of Justice, Cures 2.0 Program. If Plaintiff does not so stipulate, then the Department of Justice may release the subpoena CURES data to Defendant but such records must be designated Attorneys' Eyes Only or, if there is no AEO designation under the existing Protective Order, must be designated Confidential but only Defendant's outside counsel, experts, and support staff/vendors may have access to them and may access them solely for the purposes of this case. This order makes no ruling on the evidentiary impact of a CCP 2032.320(c) stipulation in this case. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mpritt@bsfllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO MONTEAGUDO ET AL VS. GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Eduardo Monteagudo
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 26, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, SF 267 GREEN STREET, LLC's Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Eduardo Monteagudo. Pro Tem Judge Maxwell Pritt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion To Compel IME of Plaintiff Eduardo Monteagudo is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff Monteagudo must attend and participate in the two IMEs (forensic psychological examination and forensic psychiatric examination) set forth in Defendant's motion, except that each IME is limited to no more than four hours and must take place on separate days (and if Plaintiff prefers, in separate weeks). Any transcript or recording of the IMEs must be designated Attorneys' Eyes Only or, if there is no AEO designation under the existing Protective Order, they must be designated Confidential but only Defendant's outside counsel, experts, and support staff/vendors may have access to them and may access them solely for the purposes of this case. Alternatively, if Plaintiff stipulates to the issues set forth in California Code of Civil Procedure section 2032.320(c)(1) & (2), then Plaintiff does not need to attend or participate in either IME. Any such stipulation must take place no later than two weeks prior to the first scheduled IME. This order makes no ruling on the evidentiary impact of a CCP 2032.320(c) stipulation in this case. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mpritt@bsfllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Moption And Motion By Maxum Indemnity Company, Axis Surplus Insurance Company, And National Fire And Marine Insurance Company To Reopen Discovery To Take Three Depositions Of Motiva Enterprises, Llc; Mpa
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 26, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT MAXUM INDEMNITY COMPANY, Axis Surplus Insurance Company, And National Fire And Marine Insurance Company's Motion To Reopen Discovery To Take Three Depositions Of Motiva Enterprises, Llc. Pro Tem Judge Maxwell Pritt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion To Re-Open Discovery To Take Three Depositions of Motiva Enterprises LLC is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Discovery is re-opened for the limited purpose of taking a PMQ deposition(s) on the three topics set forth in Defendants' motion. The PMQ deposition(s) on those three topics must be limited to 9 hours total, regardless of whether Plaintiff designates separate witnesses for each topic, unless Defendants agree to pay Plaintiffs' reasonable attorneys' fees and costs in connection with those depositions, in which case Defendants may schedule the deposition on each topic for 7 hours (for a total of 21 hours). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mpritt@bsfllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: *** TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CRUZ SUPERIOR COURT *** PAMELA GERSTEIN VS. GUITARFISH MUSIC FESTIVAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue To Santa Cruz County And Request For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 26, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANTs GUITARFISH MUSIC FESTIVAL, LLC, BRENT DANA, FREDERICK MAHLER and BARNETT ENGLISH's Motion To Change Venue To Santa Cruz County And Request For Attorney Fees Defendants' motion to change venue to Santa Cruz County is GRANTED. When, as here, no special rule applies, venue is proper where any defendant resides. (CCP §395; Brown v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 482, n.6, ("when a plaintiff brings an action against several defendants, both individual and corporate, in a county in which none of the defendants reside, an individual defendant has the right to change venue to the county of his or her residence. This is true even though the action was initially brought in a county where the corporate defendants may be sued under Code of Civil Procedure section 395.5.").) This law applies when at least one cause of action supports change of venue. (Rosen v. Kessler (1956) 145 Cal.App.2d 676, 683.) I find no bad faith by plaintiff, as laws on this topic can be opaque, so the request for attorney fees and costs is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
